COMM - Communication

COMM619 Consecutive Interpretation (1-6 Credits) Builds upon the systematic, reflective approach to interpreting in real- world settings introduced in Fundamentals of Interpreting. Consecutive interpreting skills are consolidated through individual and group practice, and any remaining challenges related to consecutive interpreting sub- skills are identiﬁed and addressed. Stresses effective analysis, note- taking, and use of voice during exercises in sight translation, dialogue, and formal consecutive interpreting. Prerequisite: COMM609; and must have completed an admissions test for ﬁrst year courses; and permission of ARHU-Communication department. Repeatable to: 8 credits.
